summary.quantification_method: Relative quantification using internal standard (bovine insulin)

primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison between disease and matched control cohorts.
evidence: Comparison of the plasma samples for relative levels of selected peptides showed clear separation between the glucagonoma and the insulinoma and control samples.
a large sample batch (68 plasmas) from control subjects, and aliquots from subjects harbouring two different types of pancreatic neuroendocrine tumour (insulinoma and glucagonoma), were analysed

primary_variables.matched_definition: Intervention using a compound that inhibits a target protein, pathway, or process.
evidence: analysis of the pre- and post-octreotide treatment samples from Case study 1 showed that treatment with octreotide caused the plasma level of all peptides displayed in Figure 3A to fall after 4 days.
A trial of octreotide (100 μg thrice daily), a short acting somatostatin analogue
analysis of the pre- and post-octreotide treatment samples from Case study 1 showed that treatment with octreotide caused the plasma level of all peptides displayed in Figure 3A to fall after 4 days.

replicate_hints: the glucagonoma sample was extracted in duplicate
Plasma from the subject with insulinoma (Case study 3) was extracted in duplicate

evidence: clear separation between the glucagonoma and the insulinoma and control samples.
analysis of the pre- and post-octreotide treatment samples from Case study 1 showed that treatment with octreotide caused the plasma level of all peptides displayed in Figure 3A to fall after 4 days.
the combined analysis of peptides derived from proglucagon and CHGA could potentially be used to help distinguish a glucagonoma from other types of neuroendocrine tumour.

quantification_strategy: Relative quantification using internal standard (bovine insulin)

base_candidates_ranked.reason: Untargeted peptidomics using DDA (top 10 ions) and label-free quantification via peak area ratios to an internal standard (bovine insulin).

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: Explicit use of 'no-digest setting' in PEAKS software to identify endogenous peptides up to 65 amino acids.
